untranslatable_reason: Signal requires measurement of 'personal fundamental analysis conviction (>65% confidence)' and 'consensus correlation coefficient degradation (>15% from historical average)'. These are macro-level sentiment and correlation metrics that cannot be expressed using only price-based technical indicators (OHLCV, moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger Bands, MACD, ADX, Stochastic, Donchian, Pivots, Volume Ratio, VIX). The signal fundamentally requires external data sources (sentiment indices, correlation matrices, fundamental analysis scores) not available in the allowed indicator set.
translation_notes: SCHWAGER_DRY_0_1 is a regime/conviction-based contrarian signal requiring: (1) quantified fundamental conviction vs consensus view, (2) historical correlation degradation tracking, (3) narrative age measurement, and (4) dynamic position sizing. None of these can be operationalized with technical indicators alone. The source text references Volcker/Fed policy (macro context), not technicals. Consider: (a) enriching the backtest engine with sentiment/correlation data feeds, or (b) implementing this as a manual/discretionary overlay rule rather than automated signal.
